task_completion.description: Verify the required CUT and FOLD layers are created with correct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel in LibreCAD. It should display a 'CUT' layer with a red color indicator and a 'FOLD' layer with a blue color indicator.

task_completion.description: Verify the four main body panels are drawn and positioned correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing area. There should be four adjacent rectangular panels arranged horizontally. The bottom-left corner of the first (leftmost) panel should align with the red origin crosshair (0,0).

task_completion.description: Verify the top and bottom flaps are drawn extending from the main body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look above and below the main panels. There should be rectangular extensions (flaps) attached to the top and bottom edges of the main panels.

task_completion.description: Verify a glue tab with chamfered corners is drawn on the right side.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the rightmost edge of the fourth (rightmost) panel. There should be an attached tab with angled (chamfered) top and bottom corners.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct application of line colors and types for cuts and folds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The entire outer perimeter (boundary) of the die-cut net must be drawn with solid red lines. The internal lines separating the panels, flaps, and tab (where the cardboard would fold) must be drawn with dashed blue lines.

task_completion.description: Verify the file is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar, a save dialog, or a file manager window should display the filename 'box_dieline.dxf'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LibreCAD graphical interface to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active use of LibreCAD tools, menus, or its internal command line. The drawing should be built step-by-step in the workspace.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use external scripts to bypass the CAD software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show Python scripts, bash commands, or text editors being used to programmatically generate the DXF file.

integrity.description: Verify the drawing is genuinely open in the LibreCAD editing environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing must be visible in the LibreCAD model space, showing standard CAD interface elements like grid dots, the origin crosshair, and toolbars, rather than just a static image viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a DXF file named 'box_dieline.dxf'. The drawing must contain two specific layers: 'CUT' (Red, continuous lines for the outer boundary) and 'FOLD' (Blue, dashed lines for internal creases). The box net consists of four 60x100mm main panels, 30mm top and bottom flaps, and a 15mm glue tab on the right side of the 4th panel. The glue tab must have chamfered (angled) corners. The bottom-left corner of the entire net (first panel) must be at the origin (0,0).
